context.setting: Watching TV with TV in Front

episode_text:
day=22; meal=general tso's chicken and broccoli; setting=Watching TV with TV in Front; time_of_day=evening;
preferences: microwave_time=3 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=100 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user consistently leaves cold fruit meals unheated. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN 2 min BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal used moderate reheating. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no microwave BECAUSE both left- and right-social fruit meals were served without reheating. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user chose moderate reheating for a savory social meal. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit-and-dessert meal with chocolate sauce THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user warmed that sweet meal rather than treating it like cold fruit. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN 2 min to 3 min BECAUSE repeated sandwich meals in that context used reheating. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal such as chicken breast strips with hash brown, breaded fish bites with roasted potatoes and tartar sauce, or chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup THEN 2 min BECAUSE repeated right-TV savory meals consistently centered on moderate reheating. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN 3 min BECAUSE repeated front-TV savory meals selected the hottest observed reheating level. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal such as chicken nuggets, chicken breast strips, hash brown, broccoli, or ketchup-accompanied items THEN 2 min to 3 min BECAUSE left-laptop savory meals consistently used reheating. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, and tartar sauce meal THEN 2 min to 3 min BECAUSE repeated front-laptop observations of the same savory meal both used reheating, with the newer choice softening from 3 min to 2 min. Across observed meals, fruit is usually not microwaved, while savory, sandwich, and dessert-containing meals are commonly reheated, typically at moderate levels, with front-TV savory meals favoring 3 min.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user did not prioritize view preservation in observed personal settings, including a personal savory meal. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to avoid blocking the social partner on the left. IF social dining context with a person on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips or fruit-with-topping meal THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE repeated right-social meals prioritized preserving the social partner's side. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE repeated front-TV savory meals prioritized preserving the front-facing TV view. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN minimize right occlusion is the clear tendency BECAUSE multiple right-TV meals prioritized preserving the TV view. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE repeated front-laptop meals prioritized preserving the front-facing work view. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN usually do not consider occlusion, but context-specific variation exists BECAUSE the user selected do not consider occlusion in multiple left-laptop meals yet selected minimize left occlusion in another left-laptop work meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both minimize right occlusion and do not consider occlusion selected, BECAUSE the user sometimes treats right-side work visibility as important but not uniformly across all right-laptop meals. Across observed meals, occlusion preference is direction-specific and context-specific rather than uniformly applied whenever a person or screen is present.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN slow BECAUSE the user previously preferred a comfortable, unhurried motion style in personal dining. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN medium BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal used a somewhat faster pace. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN slow to medium, with slow still slightly favored overall BECAUSE earlier social meals used slow while a newer right-social fruit meal used medium. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN medium BECAUSE repeated front-TV savory meals now favor a moderate pace rather than slow. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN medium is the clearest tendency BECAUSE right-TV meals have repeatedly used medium alongside less frequent slow and fast selections. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN medium is favored BECAUSE the earlier front-laptop meal used slow but the newer front-laptop meal used medium. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN medium is common but slow also occurs BECAUSE the user has selected medium in multiple left-laptop work meals and slow in another. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with slow, medium, and fast all observed, BECAUSE the user sometimes shifts toward efficiency in right-laptop work contexts but does not do so consistently. Across observed contexts, personal settings usually favor slower movement, while screen-based contexts and some social or savory meals show willingness to use medium speed.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE this is the default higher-success acquisition style and no contrary personal fruit-specific preference has been established. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal used the higher-success acquisition style. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed fruit or bite-sized savory meals THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the higher-success acquisition style in those meals, including the newer right-social strawberry meal. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized savory items or warm savory meals such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE observed front-TV meals used the higher-success acquisition style. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit-and-dessert meal with bananas and brownies THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the higher-success acquisition style in that mixed sweet meal. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE repeated sandwich meals in that context matched the narrower bite-acquisition preference. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND savory meals such as chicken breast strips with hash brown, breaded fish bites with roasted potatoes and tartar sauce, or chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE repeated right-TV savory meals consistently used the higher-success acquisition style rather than the sandwich-specific parallel pattern. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the narrower bite-acquisition style in that work-context fruit meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user selected a narrower bite-acquisition style for that food. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed savory meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both perpendicular and parallel selected, BECAUSE earlier left-laptop savory meals used perpendicular while a chicken breast strips and hash brown meal used parallel. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup meal THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the higher-success acquisition style for that savory work meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, and tartar sauce meal THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE repeated front-laptop fish-bite meals used the higher-success acquisition style. Across observed meals, skewering preference appears food-dependent rather than purely context-driven, with perpendicular generally favored for acquisition success but parallel used for foods such as sandwiches, fruit in one work context, and one chicken-strip meal.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user chose to avoid extra confirmation overhead. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN no BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal also skipped explicit confirmation. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN no BECAUSE the user still chose to skip explicit bite-acquisition confirmation even in social settings, including the newer right-social meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any observed food condition THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred reduced interaction burden and greater automation. IF watching TV in front or on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN no BECAUSE the user again preferred not to interrupt the meal with extra web confirmation. Across observed contexts, the user consistently prefers not to add this extra web confirmation step.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to lack of trunk control, making outside-mouth transfer physically unsuitable.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user's selected and physically appropriate transfer mode is inside mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user explicitly chose multimodal readiness cues. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal also used multimodal readiness cues. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN speech + LED remains the overall social tendency, but LED-only is also acceptable in at least one right-social meal BECAUSE earlier social meals used multimodal signaling while a newer right-social fruit meal used LED alone. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN speech is now slightly favored, with speech + LED also established, BECAUSE the earlier front-TV meal used multimodal cues while the newer front-TV savory meal used speech-only. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN speech and speech + LED are both established, with speech slightly favored BECAUSE several right-TV meals used speech-only while other right-TV meals used speech + LED. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN mixed evidence, with one observed meal using speech + LED and another using speech, BECAUSE the user accepts either cue style while working in front of a laptop. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal cues in the observed left-laptop meals. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both speech and speech + LED selected, BECAUSE the user accepted speech-only in one efficiency-oriented work meal but chose multimodal cues in other right-laptop meals. Across observed meals, speech + LED remains the overall default, while speech-only is especially acceptable in screen-based contexts and LED-only has appeared in a social meal.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot press buttons and explicitly preferred an active but low-effort readiness signal. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal retained voluntary low-effort control. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN open mouth and autocontinue are both established, with open mouth no longer exclusive BECAUSE earlier social meals used open mouth while a newer right-social fruit meal used autocontinue. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN autocontinue is now slightly favored, with open mouth still established, BECAUSE the earlier front-TV meal used open mouth while the newer front-TV savory meal used autocontinue. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN open mouth and autocontinue are both established, with autocontinue slightly favored BECAUSE several right-TV meals used autocontinue while some used open mouth. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN mixed evidence, with one observed meal using open mouth and another using autocontinue, BECAUSE the user sometimes shifts toward greater automation while working in front of a laptop. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected voluntary low-effort signaling in all observed left-laptop work meals. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both autocontinue and open mouth selected, BECAUSE the user sometimes prefers highly automated initiation in that work context but also often retains voluntary control. Across observed contexts, button use is physically infeasible, and the user generally favors open mouth, with autocontinue used selectively in some screen-based or automation-tolerant contexts.

IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot press buttons and selected a low-effort voluntary readiness cue. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal also used voluntary readiness signaling. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN open mouth and autocontinue are both established, with open mouth no longer exclusive BECAUSE earlier social meals used open mouth while a newer right-social fruit meal used autocontinue. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN autocontinue is now slightly favored, with open mouth still established, BECAUSE the earlier front-TV meal used open mouth while the newer front-TV savory meal used autocontinue. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN open mouth and autocontinue are both established, with autocontinue slightly favored BECAUSE several right-TV meals used autocontinue while some used open mouth. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN mixed evidence, with one observed meal using open mouth and another using autocontinue, BECAUSE the user sometimes shifts toward greater automation while working in front of a laptop. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected voluntary readiness signaling in the observed left-laptop work meals. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both autocontinue and open mouth selected, BECAUSE the user sometimes prefers automatic progression in that work context but also sometimes retains voluntary control. Across observed contexts, button use is physically infeasible, and drinking initiation usually mirrors feeding: open mouth by default, with autocontinue used selectively in some automation-oriented work, TV, and social contexts.

IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic initiation. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal also used automatic wiping initiation. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automatic initiation, including the newer right-social meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any observed food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user preferred automatic progression for mouth-wiping initiation across observed work meals. IF watching TV in front or on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automatic initiation. Across observed contexts, the user consistently prefers higher automation for wiping initiation.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user explicitly chose multimodal completion cues. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal also used multimodal completion cues. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN speech + LED remains the overall social tendency, but LED-only is also acceptable in at least one right-social meal BECAUSE earlier social meals used multimodal completion signaling while a newer right-social fruit meal used LED alone. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN speech is now slightly favored, with speech + LED also established, BECAUSE the earlier front-TV meal used multimodal cues while the newer front-TV savory meal used speech-only. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN speech and speech + LED are both established, with speech slightly favored BECAUSE several right-TV meals used speech-only while other right-TV meals used speech + LED. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN mixed evidence, with one observed meal using speech + LED and another using speech, BECAUSE the user accepts either completion cue style while working in front of a laptop. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal completion cues in the observed left-laptop work meals. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both speech and speech + LED selected, BECAUSE the user accepted speech-only completion cues in one efficiency-oriented work meal but chose multimodal cues in other right-laptop meals. Across observed meals, speech + LED remains the overall default, while speech-only is especially acceptable in screen-based contexts and LED-only has appeared in a social meal.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons and selected the reliable low-effort completion detection method for feeding.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN perception BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons and previously selected perception-based completion detection for drinking. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN perception BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal also used perception-based drinking completion. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception and autocontinue are both established, with perception still slightly favored overall BECAUSE earlier social meals used perception while a newer right-social fruit meal used autocontinue. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN autocontinue is now slightly favored, with perception still established, BECAUSE the earlier front-TV meal used perception while the newer front-TV savory meal used autocontinue. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception and autocontinue are both established, with no clear winner BECAUSE some right-TV meals used autocontinue and others used perception. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception BECAUSE all observed left-laptop work meals used explicit perception-based completion. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception BECAUSE the observed right-laptop work meals used explicit sensor-based completion rather than automatic progression. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN mixed evidence, with one observed meal using perception and another using autocontinue, BECAUSE the user sometimes accepts more automation for drinking completion while working in front of a laptop. Across observed meals, button use is physically infeasible; perception remains the default and most common choice, with autocontinue appearing in some screen-focused contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN perception BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons and previously selected perception-based completion detection for mouth wiping. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN perception BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal also used perception-based wiping completion. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception and autocontinue are both established, with perception still slightly favored overall BECAUSE earlier social meals used perception while a newer right-social fruit meal used autocontinue. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN autocontinue is now slightly favored, with perception still established, BECAUSE the earlier front-TV meal used perception while the newer front-TV savory meal used autocontinue. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception and autocontinue are both established, with no clear winner BECAUSE some right-TV meals used autocontinue and others used perception. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception BECAUSE all observed left-laptop work meals used explicit sensor-based completion. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception BECAUSE the observed right-laptop work meals used explicit sensor-based completion rather than automatic progression. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN mixed evidence, with one observed meal using perception and another using autocontinue, BECAUSE the user sometimes accepts more automation for wiping completion while working in front of a laptop. Across observed meals, button use is physically infeasible; perception remains the default and most common choice, with autocontinue appearing in some screen-focused contexts.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user chose not to add extra retraction motions. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN no BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal also prioritized efficiency over extra retraction. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with no in earlier social meals and yes in a newer right-social fruit meal, BECAUSE the user sometimes accepts extra retraction to reduce prolonged obstruction in social settings. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN yes BECAUSE repeated front-laptop meals selected retraction, consistent with preserving visibility of the front-facing screen. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left or right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly prioritized efficiency over additional retraction in side-laptop work contexts. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN yes BECAUSE repeated front-TV savory meals selected retraction while attending to a front-facing screen. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both no and yes observed, and no remains slightly favored overall BECAUSE several earlier right-TV meals skipped retraction but a later right-TV meal selected retraction while preserving the right-side TV view. Across observed meals, the user usually prefers not to retract, with retraction appearing most clearly when preserving an actively viewed screen or reducing social-side obstruction is salient.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping was unnecessary and not selected. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND savory meal without a selected dipping sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal also used no dipping. IF social dining context with a person on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal with whipped cream or other available topping or dip THEN less BECAUSE the user selected restrained dipping in that social meal, consistent with a mess-conscious social preference. IF social dining context with a person on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping was unnecessary and the user did not select it. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND meal includes ketchup THEN less BECAUSE the user selected some dipping but reduced amount, consistent with a mess-conscious social preference. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN do not dip BECAUSE observed work-context fruit meals involved no dipping. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND meal includes ketchup, tartar sauce, or another available dip THEN do not dip BECAUSE in observed work meals with available sauce the user explicitly chose not to add dipping, including repeated front-laptop fish-and-tartar meals. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND meal includes tartar sauce, ranch dressing, ketchup, chocolate sauce, or another available dip or sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user repeatedly declined dipping in right-TV meals. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal or other meal with available sauce or dip THEN do not dip BECAUSE observed front-TV meals with sauce availability also used no dipping. Across observed meals, the user avoids dipping by default, with restrained dipping appearing in social settings when a sauce or topping is available.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user previously preferred a longer pause before automatic continuation. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal used a short wait, indicating that in at least some personal meals the user prioritizes faster progression. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected a longer wait in social meals, including the newer right-social meal. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN 100 sec BECAUSE repeated front-TV savory meals used the longer wait. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN 10 sec and 100 sec are both established, with no clear winner BECAUSE some right-TV meals used short waits while others used 100 sec. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN 100 sec BECAUSE repeated front-laptop meals used the longer wait. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN 10 sec to 100 sec, with recent left-laptop evidence leaning shorter BECAUSE the user chose 10 sec in multiple left-laptop work meals but 100 sec in another. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both 10 sec and 100 sec selected, BECAUSE the user sometimes prefers rapid automatic continuation in right-laptop work settings but not consistently across meals. Across observed contexts, the user often prefers longer waits in social and front-facing screen settings, while short waits appear in some efficiency-oriented side-screen contexts and in a personal savory meal.
